summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened by reading Psalm 51, David's prayer of repentance after his grave sins of adultery and murder. He used this passage to introduce the sermon's central theme: being washed clean by God. Pastor Travis explained that the church often avoids talking about sin, but understanding it is crucial because it is the very reason Jesus died. He taught on the three Hebrew words for sin found in Scripture: 'chata' (missing the mark), 'avon' (distorting what is good), and 'pesha' (betraying a relationship). He connected this to the good news of Romans 6:23, that while the wages of sin is death, the free gift of God is eternal life through Jesus. The core of the message was that we cannot cleanse ourselves from sin's guilt and shame through therapy or effort; only the blood of Jesus, shed on the cross, can wash us clean. This cleansing is symbolized through water baptism. Pastor Travis spent the latter part of the sermon explaining the biblical meaning of baptism. He clarified that it is an immersion, not a sprinkling, and outlined four key purposes: it is a symbol of our death to sin and resurrection to new life; a symbol of being washed clean; a public declaration of faith; and a celebration of joining God's family. He passionately invited believers who had not been baptized to take that step, especially during the upcoming Good Friday services, and led the congregation in a prayer of surrender for those ready to give their lives to Christ.
mainPoints: Sin is defined in Scripture as missing God's mark, distorting what is good, and betraying relationship, and its eternal consequence is separation from God.
Forgiveness and cleansing from sin are a free gift from God, made possible only through the sacrificial blood of Jesus Christ shed on the cross.
Water baptism is the biblical, public symbol for a believer, representing death to sin, cleansing, public faith, and initiation into God's family.

versesMentioned.text: Have mercy on me, O God, according to your unfailing love; according to your great compassion blot out my transgressions. Wash away all my iniquity and cleanse me from my sin.
For I know my transgressions, and my sin is always before me. Against you, you only, have I sinned and done what is evil in your sight; so you are right in your verdict and justified when you judge.
Surely I was sinful at birth, sinful from the time my mother conceived me.
Cleanse me with hyssop, and I will be clean; wash me, and I will be whiter than snow.
Create in me a pure heart, O God, and renew a steadfast spirit within me.
For all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God.
You will again have compassion on us; you will tread our sins underfoot and hurl all our iniquities into the depths of the sea.
But he was pierced for our transgressions, he was crushed for our iniquities; the punishment that brought us peace was on him, and by his wounds we are healed.
For the wages of sin is death, but the gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.
Peter replied, "Repent and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins. And you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit."
Those who accepted his message were baptized, and about three thousand were added to their number that day.
And now what are you waiting for? Get up, be baptized and wash your sins away, calling on his name.
Therefore go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit.
For we were all baptized by one Spirit so as to form one body—whether Jews or Gentiles, slave or free—and we were all given the one Spirit to drink.
For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.

Monday.scripture: Psalm 51:1-2 (NIV) - "Have mercy on me, O God, according to your unfailing love... Wash away all my iniquity and cleanse me from my sin."
Monday.action: Take a moment in prayer to honestly acknowledge one area where you have 'missed the mark' this week.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you for your unfailing love. I confess my need for your cleansing. Have mercy on me and wash me clean.

Tuesday.scripture: Romans 3:23 (NIV) - "For all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God."
Tuesday.action: Reflect on the truth that no one is perfect. Release any judgmental thoughts toward others today.
Tuesday.prayer: God, help me to see myself and others through your eyes of grace, remembering we all fall short and need your salvation.

Wednesday.scripture: Romans 6:23 (NIV) - "For the wages of sin is death, but the gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord."
Wednesday.action: Write down or say out loud: "My salvation is a free gift from God, paid for by Jesus." Thank Him for this gift.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, thank you for paying the price for my sin. I receive your free gift of eternal life and forgiveness today.

Thursday.scripture: Acts 2:38 (NIV) - "Repent and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins."
Thursday.focus: The call to repentance and baptism.
Thursday.action: If you are a believer but have never been baptized, pray about taking that step. If you have been baptized, thank God for that public declaration.
Thursday.prayer: Holy Spirit, if you are prompting me to be baptized, give me courage to obey. Thank you for the symbol of my new life in Christ.

Friday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 12:13 (NIV) - "For we were all baptized by one Spirit so as to form one body..."
Friday.action: Intentionally connect with another believer today—send an encouraging text, make a call, or attend a church gathering.
Friday.prayer: Father, thank you for making me part of your family. Help me to actively love and build up my brothers and sisters in Christ.

Saturday.scripture: Psalm 51:10 (NIV) - "Create in me a pure heart, O God, and renew a steadfast spirit within me."
Saturday.action: Prepare your heart for worship. Spend a few quiet minutes asking God to renew your spirit and give you a clean heart.
Saturday.prayer: God, create in me a clean heart. Renew a right and steadfast spirit within me as I prepare to worship you.
